name: Steamed Pork Buns
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine Hoi sin sauce, oyster sauce, soy sauce and sesame oil.
2. Chop pork and onions finely.
3. Heat vegetable oil in a wok or frying pan over high heat. Stir-fry ginger and garlic in the oil for 1 minute.
4. Stir in the Hoi sin mixture. Cook and stir for two minutes.
5. Combine half cup of water and cornstarch. Mix into the Hoi sin mixture. Bring to a boil and simmer for 2 minutes. Stir in pork and onions. Remove from heat and let cool completely.
6. In a large bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and salt. Cut or rub in shortening until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs.
7. Combine remaining three quarters of cup water and vinegar. Mix with the flour until the dough sticks together. Shape into a ball and cover. Let stand for 20 minutes.
8. Uncover and knead an additional 4-5 times. Divide dough into 12 equal portions.
9. Shape each portion into a smooth ball.
10. Roll each ball of dough on a lightly floured surface into a circle 5 to 6 inches in diameter. Brush the edges lightly with water.
11. Spoon a heaping tablespoon of pork mixture onto the center of each circle. Carefully pinch edges together to seal dough around filling.
12. Fold the two ends of dough over the seam and pinch together.
13. Cut waxed paper into twelve 5-inch squares. Brush one side of paper lightly with oil.
14. Place a bun, seam side down, on each square.
15. Steam buns until done about 20 minutes.
Yield: 1 dozen
